raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I received a letter from Dillards/Citi confirming that their fraud investigation regarding my Dillards Credit Card account was completed. The letter stated that all disputed fraudulent charges, along with any related interest and fees, were removed from my account. 

However, despite this written confirmation, the charges and related fees were not actually removed. They are still being reported to credit bureaus, which has significantly lowered my credit score and is currently jeopardizing my ability to secure a mortgage loan. 

I have contacted Citi multiple times over the past several weeks, which is documented in their internal call notes, including a conversation with a XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX on XX/XX/year>. She told me that she would contact the XXXX and resolve the issue within XXXX hours. It has now been more than a week since that conversation, and no action has been taken. I have still not received confirmation that the charges were removed or that accurate information has been sent to the credit bureaus. 

This issue has now gone unresolved for over XXXX months ( since XX/XX/year> ), and I believe Citi may be violating : The Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) [ 15 U.S.C. 1666 ], by failing to correct the billing error after stating it was resolved ; The Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) [ 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) ], by continuing to report inaccurate information to credit bureaus after acknowledging that it was fraudulent. 

Thanks, XXXX XXXX
